title: ilex sideroxyloides subsp. occidentalis
intro: Ilex sideroxyloides subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Mexican Holly, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Mexico and Central America. It is a shrub or small tree that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Mexican Holly include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce blood pressure. Mexican Holly can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
